From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

2006 Canadian film

| Flutter | |
| --- | --- |
| Directed by | [Howie Shia](/wiki/Howie_Shia "Howie Shia") |
| Written by | Howie Shia |
| Produced by | Michael Fukushima Howie Shia |
| Music by | Howie Shia |
| Distributed by | [National Film Board of Canada](/wiki/National_Film_Board_of_Canada "National Film Board of Canada") |
| Release date | * 2006 (2006) |
| Running time | 6 minutes 52 seconds |
| Country | [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada") |

***Flutter*** is a 2006 Canadian [anime](/wiki/Anime "Anime")-style [animated short](/wiki/Animated_short "Animated short") by [Howie Shia](/wiki/Howie_Shia "Howie Shia"), co-produced by PPF House and the [National Film Board of Canada](/wiki/National_Film_Board_of_Canada "National Film Board of Canada").

## Awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flutter_(2006_film)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Awards")]

*Flutter* received the [Open Entries Grand Prize at the Tokyo Anime Awards](/wiki/Tokyo_Anime_Award#Open_Entries_Grand_Prize "Tokyo Anime Award") — the first work from outside Asia to win this award.[[1]](#cite_note-Leader-1) *Flutter* also received the award for best Quebec short feature at the [Fantasia Festival](/wiki/Fantasia_Festival "Fantasia Festival") and a Jury Award from the [Golden Sheaf Awards](/wiki/Golden_Sheaf_Awards "Golden Sheaf Awards").[[2]](#cite_note-NFB-2)[[3]](#cite_note-Fantasia-3)

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flutter_(2006_film)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Plot")]

A quiet film without color, a boy and a girl must abandon their friendship to pursue their dreams. In this short animation, a young boy takes a flying leap away from normal, waves goodbye to his classmates, and disappears into the cityscape and beyond. At the same time, a young girl is inspired to reinvent her space with art.[[2]](#cite_note-NFB-2)

## Production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flutter_(2006_film)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Production")]

Shia, who grew up in [Saskatchewan](/wiki/Saskatchewan "Saskatchewan"), was in [Montreal](/wiki/Montreal "Montreal") when he was approached by NFB producer [Michael Fukushima](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Fukushima&action=edit&redlink=1 "Michael Fukushima (page does not exist)") and asked if he had any ideas he wanted to present. He offered a few ideas before remembering some earlier art work he had done of a "kid with wings on his feet" while working with the NFB on his first project, a 30-second short *Ice Ages*.[[4]](#cite_note-ice-4) This artwork became the inspiration for *Flutter*.[[5]](#cite_note-Castleman-5)[[6]](#cite_note-Reel_interview-6)

*Flutter* is animated in black and white. It took Shia and his team 10 months to complete and was animated using [Photoshop](/wiki/Photoshop "Photoshop"), which the filmmaker used because of his unfamiliarity with [animation software](/wiki/Animation_software "Animation software") at the time, and because he wanted the film to "look as hand-drawn and gritty as possible."[[5]](#cite_note-Castleman-5)

Several people who had grown up with Shia in [Saskatoon](/wiki/Saskatoon "Saskatoon") worked on *Flutter*, including his brothers Leo and Tim, who played music for the film, and friends Kelly Sommerfield ([background art](/wiki/Background_artist "Background artist")) and Ryan Patterson ([sound engineering](/wiki/Sound_engineering "Sound engineering")).[[1]](#cite_note-Leader-1)
